title: lithocarpus coopertus
intro: Lithocarpus coopertus, also known as the California tanbark oak or tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. Traditionally, the plant's bark has been used in herbal medicine, containing tannins that exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus coopertus have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Lithocarpus coopertus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to promote health and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
